architecture.summary: Extract resolveIncrementId() from evaluate-completion.ts to a shared utility at src/utils/resolve-increment-id.ts. Change complete command from single-arg to variadic. No new modules or architectural patterns — follows existing patterns from archive command.

integrations.summary: No new integrations. The complete command already triggers GitHub/JIRA/ADO sync via StatusChangeSyncTrigger and LifecycleHookDispatcher. Batch mode calls the same completeIncrement() per ID — sync behavior unchanged.

ui-ux.summary: CLI-only changes. Short-ID: users type fewer characters. Batch mode: single command for multiple IDs. Error messages follow existing chalk-colored pattern. Summary line added at end of batch runs showing pass/fail counts.

performance.summary: N/A — CLI command, not hot path. Batch mode processes sequentially (not parallel). resolveIncrementId() does a single readdirSync which is already done in evaluate-completion. No performance concerns.

security.summary: Short-ID resolution uses the same validateIncrementId() for the resolved full slug — path traversal protection preserved. The resolver only searches within .specweave/increments/ directory. No new user input vectors beyond existing increment ID validation.

edge-cases.summary: Ambiguous short-IDs (multiple matches) fail with listed matches. Zero matches fail with clear error. Batch mode: one failure does not stop others. AC validator fix: parseTasksWithUSLinks called with path instead of content — existing try/catch handles remaining errors. Already-completed increments return true (not error) per existing behavior.
